title: Controller set to address(0) permanently locks all funds
reason: Setting an invalid controller is a governance-only misconfiguration, and governance can later set a valid controller again; the claim of permanent lock is overstated and not a standalone protocol bug.

title: Controller can drain any ERC20 tokens from vault via harvest()
reason: `harvest()` is an explicitly privileged controller-only sweep for non-underlying tokens; this is a trust/roles assumption, not an unprivileged exploit.

title: Division by zero in withdraw when totalSupply is zero
reason: A meaningful withdraw cannot occur when `totalSupply()` is zero because no user holds shares then; this is only a trivial edge-case revert such as `withdraw(0)`.

title: Division by zero in deposit when _pool is zero
reason: The stated path is incorrect because if all users fully withdraw then `totalSupply()` also becomes zero, taking the first-deposit branch. The remaining `_pool == 0 && totalSupply() > 0` case requires prior total asset loss/insolvency, not a new logic flaw in `deposit()`.

title: setMin can be set to 0, disabling yield generation
reason: This is a governance-controlled configuration choice rather than an exploitable vulnerability.

title: No slippage protection allows sandwich attacks on deposit/withdraw
reason: The claim is too generic for this share vault design and does not show a concrete profitable manipulation beyond the separate donation/inflation issue already captured.

title: No deadline parameter allows unfavorable transaction execution
reason: This is a generic UX/property complaint, not a concrete protocol vulnerability in the vault logic.

title: setController has no validation allowing malicious controller
reason: Choosing the controller is a governance trust boundary; governance can already point the vault at any controller, so this does not introduce an additional permissionless exploit.

title: earn() can be called by anyone triggering unnecessary operations
reason: Subsumed by the stronger finding that repeated public `earn()` calls can drain the local withdrawal buffer to dust.

title: Missing ERC20 transfer return value check in deposit (indirect)
reason: Incorrect: `deposit()` uses `SafeERC20.safeTransferFrom`, which already checks call success and optional return values.
